name: Auto-format JS/TS on edit (Prettier)
description: After Claude writes or edits a file, run Prettier on that exact file so the diff is always clean. Keeps you from reviewing formatting noise.
note: Requires `jq` and `npx prettier` available. On Windows, run Claude Code from Git Bash/WSL for this shell form, or adapt the command.

hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | grep -Eq '\.(js|jsx|ts|tsx|css|scss|json|md|html|vue|svelte)$' && jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | xargs -r npx --no-install prettier --write 2>/dev/null; exit 0

name: Auto-fix Python on edit (Ruff)
description: After Claude edits a .py file, run `ruff --fix` and format on that file. Style stays consistent and lint errors get caught immediately, not at CI.

hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path'); case "$f" in *.py) ruff check --fix "$f" 2>/dev/null; ruff format "$f" 2>/dev/null;; esac; exit 0

name: Block dangerous shell commands
description: A PreToolUse guard that blocks catastrophic commands (recursive deletes of /, ~, or $HOME, and disk-wipe patterns) before they run. Cheap insurance for autonomous sessions.
note: Requires `jq`. Blocks via exit code 2. Tune the pattern to your needs.

hooks.command: c=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command'); echo "$c" | grep -Eq 'rm[[:space:]]+-[a-z]*r[a-z]*f?[[:space:]]+(/|~|\$HOME|\*)|mkfs|dd[[:space:]]+if=' && { echo 'loadout: blocked a dangerous command' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

name: Refuse to read secret files
description: Blocks Read on .env / credential files so secrets never get pulled into context (and can't leak into logs or commits). Good default for any repo with real credentials.

hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path'); echo "$f" | grep -Eq '(^|/)\.env($|\.)|\.pem$|id_rsa|credentials(\.json)?$' && { echo 'loadout: refusing to read a secret file' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

name: Ping me when Claude finishes
description: Rings the terminal bell (and prints a marker) when Claude stops responding, so you can tab away during long runs and come back when it's your turn.
note: The bell works in most terminals. Swap in `osascript`/`notify-send`/`powershell` for a desktop toast.

hooks.command: printf '\a' ; echo 'loadout: Claude is waiting for you.'

name: Status line: dir + git branch + model
description: A compact status line showing the current directory, git branch, and active model. Small quality-of-life upgrade you notice every session.
note: Requires `jq` and `git`. Reads the status JSON Claude Code pipes to the command on stdin.

statusLine.command: input=$(cat); dir=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd' | sed "s|$HOME|~|"); model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // "claude"'); branch=$(git -C "$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.cwd')" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); printf '%s  %s  %s' "$(basename "$dir")" "${branch:+\u2387 $branch}" "$model"
